25.name: into
25.docs: Calls `U::from(self)`. That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of [From]<T> for U chooses to do.

251.name: from_bits_truncate
251.docs: Convert from a bits value, unsetting any unknown bits.

250.name: from_bits
250.docs:
Convert from a bits value.

This method will return `None` if any unknown bits are set.

249.name: bits
249.docs:
Get the underlying bits value.

The returned value is exactly the bits set in this flags value.

123.name: to_writer
123.docs:
Write a flags value as text.

Any bits that aren't part of a contained flag will be formatted as a hex number.

274.name: ALL
274.docs: A value with all bits set.

248.name: contains_unknown_bits
248.docs: This method will return `true` if any unknown bits are set.

122.name: iter
122.docs: Yield the bits of a source flags value in a set of contained flags values.

273.name: EMPTY
273.docs: A value with all bits unset.

222.name: is_unnamed
222.docs:
Whether the flag is unnamed.

If [`Flag::name`] returns a non-empty string then this method will return `false`.

247.name: unknown_bits
247.docs: Get the unknown bits from a flags value.

272.name: complement
272.docs: The bitwise negation (`!`) of the bits in `self`, truncating the result.

221.name: is_named
221.docs:
Whether the flag is named.

If [`Flag::name`] returns a non-empty string then this method will return `true`.

246.name: known_bits
246.docs: Get the known bits from a flags value.

271.name: symmetric_difference
271.docs: The bitwise exclusive-or (`^`) of the bits in `self` and `other`.

220.name: value
220.docs: Get the flags value of this flag.

245.name: all_named
245.docs:
Get a flags value with all bits from named flags set.

This method is equivalent to [`Flags::all`] unless [`Flags::FLAGS`] contains unnamed flags.

270.name: difference
270.docs:
The intersection of `self` with the complement of `other` (`&!`).

This method is not equivalent to `self & !other` when `other` has unknown bits set.
`difference` won't truncate `other`, but the `!` operator will.

320.name: Internal
320.docs: The type of the internal field on the generated flags type.

219.name: name
219.docs:
Get the name of this flag.

If the flag is unnamed then the returned string will be empty.

244.name: all
244.docs: Get a flags value with all known bits set.

269.name: union
269.docs: The bitwise or (`|`) of the bits in `self` and `other`.

319.name: Primitive
319.docs: The type of the underlying storage.

218.name: new
218.docs:
Define a flag.

If `name` is non-empty then the flag is named, otherwise it's unnamed.

243.name: empty
243.docs: Get a flags value with all bits unset.

268.name: intersection
268.docs: The bitwise and (`&`) of the bits in `self` and `other`.

217.name: Flag
217.docs: A defined flags value that may be named or unnamed.

242.name: Bits
242.docs: A bits type that can be used as storage for a flags type.

267.name: clear
267.docs: Unsets all bits in the flags.

266.name: set
266.docs: Call [`Flags::insert`] when `value` is `true` or [`Flags::remove`] when `value` is `false`.

265.name: toggle
265.docs: The bitwise exclusive-or (`^`) of the bits in `self` and `other`.

189.name: empty_flag
189.docs: A hex or named flag wasn't found between separators.

264.name: remove
264.docs:
The intersection of `self` with the complement of `other` (`&!`).

This method is not equivalent to `self & !other` when `other` has unknown bits set.
`remove` won't truncate `other`, but the `!` operator will.

188.name: invalid_named_flag
188.docs: A named flag that doesn't correspond to any on the flags type was encountered.

213.name: PublicFlags
213.docs:
A trait for referencing the `bitflags`-owned internal type
without exposing it publicly.

263.name: insert
263.docs: The bitwise or (`|`) of the bits in `self` and `other`.

262.name: truncate
262.docs: Remove any unknown bits from the flags.

186.name: invalid_hex_flag
186.docs: An invalid hex flag was encountered.

60.name: IterNames
60.docs:
An iterator over flags values.

This iterator only yields flags values for contained, defined, named flags. Any remaining bits
won't be yielded, but can be found with the [`IterNames::remaining`] method.

211.name: parser
211.docs:
Parsing flags from text.

Format and parse a flags value as text using the following grammar:

- _Flags:_ (_Whitespace_ _Flag_ _Whitespace_)`|`*
- _Flag:_ _Name_ | _Hex Number_
- _Name:_ The name of any defined flag
- _Hex Number_: `0x`([0-9a-fA-F])*
- _Whitespace_: (\s)*

As an example, this is how `Flags::A | Flags::B | 0x0c` can be represented as text:

```text
A | B | 0x0c
```

Alternatively, it could be represented without whitespace:

```text
A|B|0x0C
```

Note that identifiers are *case-sensitive*, so the following is *not equivalent*:

```text
a|b|0x0C
```

261.name: contains
261.docs: Whether all set bits in `other` are also set in `self`.

336.name: bitflags

59.name: remaining
59.docs:
Get a flags value of any remaining bits that haven't been yielded yet.

Once the iterator has finished, this method can be used to
check whether or not there are any bits that didn't correspond
to a contained, defined, named flag remaining.

260.name: intersects
260.docs: Whether any set bits in `other` are also set in `self`.

259.name: is_all
259.docs: Whether all known bits in this flags value are set.

133.name: from_str_strict
133.docs:
Parse a flags value from text.

This function will fail on any names that don't correspond to defined flags.
This function will fail to parse hex values.

334.name: bitflags_match

82.name: IterDefinedNames
82.docs:
An iterator over all defined named flags.

This iterator will yield flags values for all defined named flags, regardless of
whether they are contained in a particular flags value.

258.name: is_empty
258.docs: Whether all bits in this flags value are unset.

132.name: to_writer_strict
132.docs: Write only the contained, defined, named flags in a flags value as text.

333.name: bitflags

257.name: iter_equal_names
257.docs:
Get an iterator over all defined names for this flags value.

This iterator will yield all defined names for the flags value, including
any convenience flags.

131.name: from_str_truncate
131.docs:
Parse a flags value from text.

This function will fail on any names that don't correspond to defined flags.
Unknown bits will be ignored.

256.name: iter_defined_names
256.docs: Yield a set of all named flags defined by [`Self::FLAGS`].

130.name: to_writer_truncate
130.docs: Write a flags value as text, ignoring any unknown bits.

54.name: Flags

255.name: iter_names
255.docs:
Yield a set of contained named flags values.

This method is like [`Flags::iter`], except only yields bits in contained named flags.
Any unknown bits, or bits not corresponding to a contained flag will not be yielded.

129.name: ParseHex
129.docs: Parse a value from a hex string.

103.name: IterEqualNames
103.docs: An iterator over all defined names for a specific flags value.

254.name: iter
254.docs:
Yield a set of contained flags values.

Each yielded flags value will correspond to a defined named flag. Any unknown bits
will be yielded together as a final flags value.

128.name: ParseError
128.docs: An error encountered while parsing flags from text.

2.name: Iter
2.docs:
An iterator over flags values.

This iterator will yield flags values for contained, defined flags first, with any remaining bits yielded
as a final flags value.

329.name: external
329.docs: Conditional trait implementations for external libraries.

253.name: from_name
253.docs:
Get a flags value with the bits of a flag with the given name set.

This method will return `None` if `name` is empty or doesn't
correspond to any named flag.

127.name: from_str
127.docs:
Parse a flags value from text.

This function will fail on any names that don't correspond to defined flags.
Unknown bits will be retained.

252.name: from_bits_retain
252.docs: Convert from a bits value exactly.

126.name: WriteHex
126.docs:
Encode a value as a hex string.

Implementors of this trait should not write the `0x` prefix.
